free_text: ANALYZING POWERS OF CONTINUUM SPECTRA OF REACTIONS
EN-SEC AND DATA SECTIONS ARE CORRECTED.
THE SI-28,NI-58 AND BI-209 TARGETS WERE SELF-SUPPORTING
METALLIC FOILS WITH THICKNESSES RANGING FROM 2 TO 44
WERE DETECTED BY A PAIR OF COUNTER TELESCOPES
POSITIONED AT SYMMETRIC ANGLES TO THE BEAM. EACH
COUNTER TELESCOPE CONSISTED OF A SI DETECTOR OF 400-MUM
THICKNESS AND HIGH-PURITY GE DETECTOR OF 15-MM
THICKNESS.  PROTONS AND DEUTRONS WERE IDENTIFIED BY THE
CYCLOTRON. THE POLARIZED PROTONS FROM THE ATOMIC BEAM
TYPICAL BEAM CURRENT WAS 50 NA ON TARGET WITH A
POLARIZATION OF 68%. THE BEAM POLARIZATION WAS REVERSED
EVERY 300 MSEC AND ITS POLARIZATION WAS MONITORED
. THIS IS UNCERTAINTY WHICH IS SHOWN ON THE
1. THE EFFECT OF TARGET THICKNESS ON ANALYZING POWERS
WAS EXAMINED BY COMPARING THE ANALYZING POWER OF A THIN
TARGET WITH THAT OF A THICK ONE AND IT WAS FOUND TO BE
2. THE BACKGROUND PRODUCED BY EDGE SCATTERING FROM THE
DETECTOR SLITS WAS ESTIMATED FROM P-P SCATTERING DATA.
SINCE THE RATIO OF AN ELASTIC PEAK TO SLIT-SCATTERED
BACKGROUND WAS 1000-10000 AT FORWARD ANGLES, WE EXPECT
NO SIGNIFICANT CONTRIBUTION FROM THE SLIT-EDGE
SCATTERING TO THE OBSERVED ANALYZING POWER VALUES.
OTHER SYSTEMATIC ERRORS CONSIDERED ARE IN ALL CASES
